segments.text: How well do you know your neighbor, your
co-workers, even your best friend? Well,
you might start secondguessing yourself
after watching Parasite: The Maxim, the
anime that takes its main character from
being an unsuspecting average Joe like
this.
>> Are you okay?
>> To a creature from your sci-fi thriller,
Nightmares.
Now that's a transformation. I've got a
good one for you today. So, let's get
right into it. The first episode opens
with an older couple standing in their
living room. There's nothing out of the
ordinary until the man's head splits
open to reveal rows of sharp teeth and
tentacles. Stranger Things just wrapped
recently. And this thing is giving the
demogorgan a run for its money. Even
worse, the poor woman just stands there
in horror while her husband bites off
her head. Now that's a cold open. I know
we all want to figure out what the hell
just went on there, but first we've got
to meet our main character, Shinichi.
The next scene opens with him asleep in
his bed while a disembodied voice tells
him that it was unfortunate that it
failed. The unfamiliar voice wakes
Shinichi and chalking it up to a bad
dream, he goes downstairs where his
parents, Kazayyuki and Nouko, are having
breakfast. They tease him about the
snake he saw in the night and how he
tied an earphone cord around his arm
while trying to catch it. First the
voice and now he had a night terror
about a snake. Man, this guy has some
weird dreams. Shinichi loses his
appetite after a spider crawls onto the
table. He's got a massive fear of bugs,
so he decides to head to school early.
On the way, one of his friends, a girl
named Marano, sneaks up on him from
behind. She tries to scare him, but he
reaches back and catches her hand
midair. When Shinichi turns around, he's
shocked to see Murano as if he didn't
just foil her plans. To make matters
worse, he grabs her chest, which earns
him a slap so strong that it knocks the
glasses from his face. I would say he
deserved it, but he genuinely seems
confused, as if he has no control over
his hand. Yeah, something is definitely
up with this guy. Another of Shinichi's
friends, a bookish girl named Yuko,
witnesses the entire thing and still
picks up his glasses for him. She asks
if there's something wrong with his
right hand, which he unconvincingly
denies. Later in class, Shinichi has a
flashback to the night before. Some sort
of alien creature tried to enter his
ear, but couldn't get past his earbuds.
It tried to go through his nose, but
Shinichi sneezed it out. When he saw the
thing, he mistook it for a snake and
tried to kill it with a book, only for
it to go into his hand. Yeah, something
tells me that this wasn't just a bad
dream.
>> What the hell is going on?
>> Shinichi's really having an off day at
school. Not only is his friend mad at
him, but he's in trouble with his
teachers for not paying attention. He
decides to call it a day and go home
early. But on his way, he encounters a
little girl chasing a ball across the
road. 5 seconds from getting hit,
Shinichi manages to save the girl and
stop the car in its tracks. Did does
this remind you guys of anything else?
No, just me. Okay. Anyway, after
Shinichi's freak show of superhuman
strength, he notices an eyeball staring
at him from his right hand, and he runs
home in terror. Back in his bedroom,
Shinichi tries stabbing his hand with a
knife. In retaliation, his right hand
transforms into an alien creature with
eyes and a mouth, but it doesn't know
how to speak beyond basic words. It
breaks the knife in half before tossing
it aside, preventing Shinichi from
hurting it. Then it goes back to sleep,
returning to the appearance of an
average human hand. Shinichi spends
hours searching for what the creature in
his hand could be, but comes up short.
Eventually, he just gives up and goes
downstairs to have dinner with his
parents. The next morning, Shinichi
wakes up to find the alien creature
protruding from his right hand while
studying biology books. The creature
explains that there is no mention of its
kind in human texts, and it doesn't know
where it came from. It does know that it
was supposed to enter and take over
Shinichi's brain, effectively killing
him and using his body as a vessel, but
it reached maturity while living in
Shinichi's hand, so it can no longer
take him over or live without him. The
creature suggests that they learn how to
coexist, so it doesn't die, and Shinichi
can keep his right arm. For some reason,
Shinichi agrees with this. Now that he's
sharing a body with a creature of
unknown origin, some things start to
change. When he goes down for breakfast,
he notably eats more than usual. He also
catches a spider and releases it
outside, overcoming his fears. More
importantly, he learns about a string of
vicious murders sweeping the nation. The
mention of murders seems to put
Shinichi's head on straight because he
decides to tell the police about his
alien hitchhiker. The organism talks him
out of it by bringing up how Shinichi
would become the government's newest
science project before it senses another
of its kind nearby, a parasite. Shinichi
finds the parasite possessing the mind
of a dog, which isn't the best host it
could have found. The parasite
transforms and tries to attack Shinichi,
but his parasite rips out the dog's
heart to kill it. The final scene of the
episode takes us back 2 days where a man
stabs a woman through the chest. He
laughs while looking toward the sky.
Around the same time, a parasite egg
lands on a satellite dish outside
Shinichi's window where it hatches. The
parasite's snake like form crawls
towards Shinichi. That's a lot to unpack
for a first episode, but it looks like
Shinichi has an alien invasion on his
hands. The second episode starts with a
parasite saving a woman from three men.
Of course, it's using a poor guy as a
vessel, so she has no idea that her
savior is a maneing alien. The parasite
offers to walk her home, only for the
three guys to catch up with them again.
Surrounded, the parasite decides to
eliminate witnesses by killing all four
of them just to be safe. Oh man. So much
for these being chivalous aliens.
Meanwhile, Shinichi is on his way to
school and he's made his peace with his
unique situation. He decides he needs to
call his parasite something and they
land on the name Migi. They also throw
the he him pronoun around, so that's
what I will be using. Later during gym
class, Shinichi is killing it in
basketball. I get the feeling he's not
the most athletic type on a typical day,
and Murano is swooning on the sidelines.
Another one of their classmates, a guy
called Nagayi, notices the sparks flying
between these two and stomps on
Shinichi's foot to throw him off his
game. Fortunately, Migi helps him get
another basket just to impress the girl.
After class, Shinichi apologizes for
inappropriately touching Murano, and she
says he can make it up to her by taking
her out for pancakes. We all agree that
she's counting that as a date, right? As
Murano leaves, Migi acts out again,
giving Shinichi a hard time for having a
crush. The teasing doesn't last long,
though, because that Nagai dude comes
back and tells Shinichi to follow him
outside. I saw this coming a mile away,
but Nagayi starts whailing on Shinichi,
saying that he's had a crush on Murano
since middle school. Dude, if that's the
case, why haven't you made a move yet?
You must know you don't stand a chance.
Anyway, Migi punches Nagayi in the face,
sending him running. Shinichi makes it
to the bathroom where he tells Migi not
to talk when other people are around.
Migi is more concerned about Shinichi's
human anatomy because alien. So Shinichi
has to book it out of the bathroom after
another student nearly walks in on a
very awkward conversation between our
dude and his right hand. Fortunately,
Shinichi manages to get home without
incident and even talks to Yuki about
the countrywide deaths, now being dubbed
the mincemeat murders. At home, Shinichi
tries to convince Migi to help him stop
the murders. When Migi disagrees because
it will needlessly endanger them both,
they have this very pleasant
interaction.
>> You're a demon,
>> Shinichi. I have done some research into
the concept of these demons you speak
of, and I would say that humans are the
life forms most closely resembling them.
>> Oh, yeah. That line's definitely going
to stick with me for a while. The next
day, Migi tells Shinichi that another
one of its kind is approaching. So,
Shinichi purposefully lures the parasite
to a park to confront it. However, when
the parasite catches up, it cuts off its
own arm to give Migi a new home. It
believes that they can share one host
and become stronger together. Even
though joining the other parasite would
mean killing Shinichi. Time stretches on
and Shinichi must be seeing his life
flash before his eyes before Migi kills
the other parasite. Of course, Migi
claims to have only done so because
there was no guarantee that transferring
to a new host would be successful. Oh,
but I know that you're just a big softy,
Migi. Sometime later, Shinichi takes
Murano out for those pancakes.
Afterward, they decide to go to the
park, but run into a group of guys
throwing rocks at a cat they buried in
the sandbox. Shinichi goes to rescue the
cat, but the teenagers throw rocks at
him. Of course, Shinichi catches the
rocks without looking and threatens to
eat them alive if they don't stop. The
teens get the hell out of Dodge. And if
Murano didn't have feelings for Shinichi
before, she definitely does now. Girl,
lock it in. Anyways, Shinichi and Murano
walk home holding hands and Murano asks
if he's really Shinichi. Uh, he says yes
and technically it's not a lie. The
episode closes with a scene of the
parasite from the opening scene standing
in a room with an unnamed woman. They're
nude. They're definitely both aliens.
And I've got a feeling we might have
some baby parasites in the near future.
Yeah. In the third episode, things are
ramping up and fast. The mince meat
murders have progressed to include 84
victims and counting, which means
there's at least as many bloodthirsty
parasites running around. While
attending school one day, Migi senses a
fellow parasite in the body of
Shinichi's new math teacher, Ryoko. Of
course, they can't spot one without
getting noticed themselves. So, Shinichi
and Riyoko find themselves in a bit of a
stalemate. Riyoko wants to blend in with
human society. So, she's not planning a
murder spree like the rest of their
species. She and Migi agree to meet
somewhere more private after school to
discuss the parasites future. At the
meeting spot, Riyoko catches Shinichi
and Migi offg guard by bringing along a
friend. Another parasite that goes by
the name A. A is the parasite from the
last episode who hooked up with that
unknown woman. Yeah, that woman is Rio.
From the start, A doesn't like or trust
Shinichi. However, they're all caught
off guard by Riyoko revealing that she's
pregnant with a human baby.
>> And the result might surprise you. I am
now pregnant, Mr. Izumi.
>> Apparently, regardless of being with
another parasite or a human, a
parasite's child will always be a normal
human. Huh. Okay. That is not a twist
that I saw coming. The informational
meeting bores a so he ends up leaving
early, slipping away from the more
level-headed aliens. The next day, he
shows himself again. Only this time,
it's to terrorize Shinichi's school. He
goes on a rampage, forcing students and
staff to evacuate the building. To
protect himself, Shinichi goes to an
unoccupied part of the school where he
builds a barricade out of tables while
Migi tells him to make use of his still
working brain by using it as a weapon.
They barely arm themselves before A
breaks past their barricade, prepared
for a fight. The fourth episode opens
where we last left off with Shinichi
facing off against A. In those brief
seconds between life and death, Shinichi
grapples with the fear overtaking his
senses. Apparently, he and Migi had made
a plan for situations like this, which
requires Migi to play defense to protect
them and Shinichi to play offense and
kill the parasite. Shinichi calms his
nerves enough to get in close and stab A
through the chest with a chair leg. It's
gnarly. Blood spills from the leg like
it's a running faucet. You can't see me
grimacing behind the screen, but take my
word for it. That's a brutal way to go.
A should die from the sheer amount of
blood loss he sustains, but he escapes,
hoping to merge with Ryoko and share her
body. Instead, Ryoko traps him in a room
and blows him up by igniting a gas leak.
After the event, Shinichi gets some time
off from school, but classes ultimately
resume. On the first day back, the staff
have a meeting to discuss the
implications of Riyoko's pregnancy and
the fact that she can't tell them who
the baby's father is. They're judging
her pretty hard. So, to avoid further
suspicion, Riyoko resigns from her
teaching position. Listen, even if she
wasn't a brain leachching parasite, I
would still support quitting after that
meeting. Anywh who, Shinichi stops
Riyoko right before she leaves campus,
but refrains from killing her because of
her human child. She then tells him that
she plans to run experiments on her
child when they're born and will eat
them when they're no longer useful. Huh,
you've gotten yourself into quite the
pickle there, haven't you, Shinichi?
Riyoko goes on to say that all species
have basic instincts, a set of rules
programmed into them from birth, and
that hers require her to devour
humankind. She then goes to kill
Shinichi, but sensing a change in him,
she lets him live. Later that night, the
real Riyoko's mother comes to visit her.
She's worried because the school called
to tell her that Riyoko resigned earlier
in the day. A mother knows things and
she's not there 5 minutes before she
realizes the thing standing in front of
her isn't her daughter.
>> It's not right. Something's very wrong.
Who are you? Byoko, where's my Rio?
>> The picture perfect replication isn't
enough. The parasites can't fake the
life in a human's eyes. Alien Riyoko
kills her mom before she can call for
help. Meanwhile, Shinichi's parents tell
him about a countryside vacation over
dinner. Obviously, Shinichi is against
them going on a trip while there are
murderous aliens on the loose. Migi
reminds him that the parasites prefer
higher traffic areas, so they should be
safe in the country. The next morning,
Shinichi apologizes for his outburst and
encourages them to go on their trip.
That's when his mom calls him out for
being different and begs him to tell her
why he's changed so much. He chalks it
up to not wanting to be lonely and
promises that he's fine. She isn't
convinced, but agrees to go on the
vacation. On the next day, they leave.
She's the last one out the door,
promising Shinichi that they'll be back
soon. Aw, those are definitely her
famous last words, aren't they? The
fifth episode begins with a couple
driving. And the woman is definitely
weird, like wrapping the seat belt
around her head because she doesn't know
how it works. Weird. Obviously, that
distracts the driver and he crashes the
car. The woman dies on impact, so the
parasite attempts to transfer itself to
the man. Unfortunately for it, moving
between bodies isn't easy. On his way to
school the following day, Migi points
out that people are starting to notice
changes in Shinichi. These changes even
make sense because fusing with an alien
should change him on a physical and
psychological level. Shinichi hates this
theory, especially because Ryoko sensed
a change in him so significant that she
let him live. As Shinichi is having an
identity crisis, he comes across his
bully Nagai, who is getting beaten up by
a guy named Mitsuo and his gang.
Shinichi thinks it's inhuman nature to
be self-sacrificial.
So, to prove a point, he goes in to
rescue Nagai. The problem is Migi is
asleep at this point. So, he's
defenseless as the gang members beat him
to a pulp, too. Fortunately, the female
student who hangs with the gang, Kana,
calls Mitsuo off because she doesn't
think it's a fair fight. Oddly enough,
when she goes to check on Shinichi, she
looks into his eyes and knows there's
something wrong with him. Even though
Kana calls the fight there, Mitzo comes
back for more the next day. except this
time he and his gang attack Murano,
scaring her and infuriating Shinichi.
Obviously, he's going to fight to save
his friend, but he gets busted up pretty
good this time, too. The difference is
that Migi is awake for this fight, and
Shinichi needs to keep his parasite from
murdering the gang members. Fortunately,
Nagai shows up in the nick of time with
a group of other students who are there
to rescue him. The gang members know
they're outnumbered, so they call it a
day. Safe again, Murano and Shinichi
walk home together, and Shinichi even
invites her to stay over since his
parents are out of town, but she
declines. Meanwhile, the parasite from
the episode's opening drags itself
across the highway. It can barely keep
itself together in this male form and
desperately searches for a female host.
That's how it ends up on the same beach
as Shinichi's parents, where his dad,
Kazuyuki, watches it behead his mom,
Nouko, in cold blood. Later, Kazuyuki
survives the wounds given to him by the
parasite and drags himself to the
nearest phone booth to call Shinichi.
He's barely coherent, but all he needs
to say is the word monster for Shinichi
to know what happened. God damn, poor
kid. The next morning, Nouko comes home
and even though Migi tells him that the
parasite is no longer his mom, he
prevents Migi from attacking her.
Meanwhile, Nouko's parasite talks about
going back to finish off Kazuyuki before
stabbing Shinichi through the heart. The
sixth episode opens with the brutal
scene of Shinichi bleeding out in his
home's hallway. With precious seconds to
spare, Migi decides to risk it all by
forming itself to act as Shinichi's
heart while simultaneously working to
repair the damaged one. It is a lengthy
process that takes a full day to
complete with Shinichi unconscious the
entire time. When he finally wakes, he
realizes that he doesn't need glasses to
see and that he has a wicked scar,
perfect for any anime protagonist. While
he was out cold, Migi kept him hydrated
with some nice sugar water. So,
Shinichi's not feeling the best when he
gets a call from Sakura Hospital.
Surprisingly, Kazuyuki managed to
survive his injuries and is in stable
condition. So, Shinichi catches the
first fairy to the island that his
parents were visiting, all while
plotting to kill the parasite that took
over Nouko. We are in his revenge arc,
ladies and gentlemen. Meanwhile,
Kazuyuki tries telling the authorities
that his wife was murdered, but they and
the hospital staff convince him that the
parasite attack was a nightmare he's
confusing for reality. In defense of the
police, Parasite Nouko went back to
their hotel, interacted with the staff,
and checked out. So, it seems to them
like she ran out on her family. Shinichi
decides that it's time to tell his dad
everything about parasites. But when he
gets to the hospital, Kazuyuki insists
Nouko's death was a dream, even though
he knows it's not true. Shinichi lets it
go and tells his dad that he'll stay
near the hotel while he recovers. Of
course, he's also sticking around to
keep an eye out for that parasite,
believing it will come back to kill
Kazuyuki soon enough. On his way to the
island, Shinichi met a girl named Makiko
on the ferry, and he stays at her
family's inn. Before going to bed that
night, Migi drops a bombshell on
Shinichi. Since he has used so much
energy to repair Shinichi's heart, he
has to sleep for 4 hours every day. And
during that time, he's unresponsive to
the outside world. That means he can't
sense other parasites or fight them off.
The next day, Shinichi is sitting
outside the hospital when Makiko comes
to sit with him. She bluntly asks if he
has a girlfriend, but then he bolts
upright because Migi senses another
parasite down by the beach. Shinichi
takes off at the speed of light. Not
really, but he's real freaking fast. And
then he leaps over the seaw wall to
reach the shore. You know, he's really
reminding me of someone. I just can't
put my finger on it. The episode ends
with Shinichi confronting the parasite
on the beach, but it's not who he
expects. In the seventh episode, we meet
a brand new character, an absolute
sweetheart of a man named Mamoru Uda. It
turns out that he's in the same boat as
Shinichi, only that his parasite lives
rentree in his mouth. On the day the
parasites landed in Japan, Mamoru
planned to take his own life by jumping
into the sea. His parasite, sensing that
Mamoru would soon drown, prioritized
helping him breathe instead of infecting
his brain. Thus, their weird
cohabitation was born. In the present,
Shinichi and Mamoru go to the hospital
roof to talk in private where Shinichi
shares his story. Mamoru cries for him
and his mom before promising to help him
locate parasite Nouko if he can. Later
that night, Migi enlightens Shinichi
with even more delightful information.
Just like Migi was changed from entering
Shinichi's heart, so was he. About 30%
of Migi entered Shinichi's bloodstream,
which is now altering him in unforeseen
ways. That's why he can run fast, jump
high distances, and even pass an eye
exam with flying colors. Shinichi
wonders if those changes include his
brain, too. I mean, he's got to ask. The
man's got to be prepared if he starts
feeling a desire to eat other people.
Also, he realizes that he hasn't cried
about losing his mom, which makes him
feel like he's losing touch with his
human side. The next day, Mamodu calls
Shinichi because he senses Nouko in town
and tracks her down. Of course, Shinichi
tells him to run before he's offed on
the spot, and our guys start running
toward each other, hoping to meet in the
middle. Unfortunately, Shinichi isn't
quick enough, and Mumoru finds himself
on a cliff edge, cornered by Nouko. They
don't stand a chance, but his parasite
realizes Nouko's fighting in a specific
pattern before she manages to stab them
in the heart, sending them tumbling over
the cliff to the beach below. On his way
to Mamoru, Shinichi's fate takes an
unfortunate turn when Migi falls asleep.
As he does, he turns Shinichi's right
arm into a blade, giving him a way to
defend himself. Even though Migi advised
him to leave and save himself, Shinichi
still goes to confront Nouko. To his
surprise, he can anticipate her every
move.
>> I can see it. I can see every move it
makes. There it is.
>> Like, come on. He's a badass now, too.
Shinichi manages to hold his own in the
fight and even gains the upper hand. But
just as he's about to deliver the
killing blow, he's overwhelmed with
flashbacks of his mom. He can't bring
himself to do it after all. But luckily,
Mamorusu sneaks up from behind, taking
her out for both of them. His parasite
had moved his vital organs out of the
way just before letting him get hit,
creating the illusion of a killing blow
so they could get in a sneak attack.
Ooh, hell yeah. We've got brains and
brawn over here. A double threat.
Anyway, the next day, Kazuyuki is
discharged from the hospital, so
Shinichi says goodbye to Mako, not
planning to see her again. Before they
leave, he and his dad stop to watch the
ocean. Kazuyuki tells him that his mom
always loved him and that she didn't
suffer in her final moments. Hearing
that and having avenged Nouko, Shinichi
collapses. Don't worry though, he's just
sleeping and it's probably the best nap
of his life. Sometime later, he returns
to school, but the Shinichi everyone
knew and loved is now an entirely new
person. In the eighth episode, we start
in a dark room with Riyoko. She's with
another parasite that's feasting on a
corpse. She tells the parasite to track
down Shinichi and keep a close eye on
him because he'll be invaluable in the
research for the future of their kind. I
don't like the sound of that. But the
parasite puts on the face of a high
schooler planning to infiltrate
Shinichi's social circle. At the same
time, Kana is loitering outside the
school to catch up with Shinichi because
his weird vibe has enchanted her. Mitzo,
the gang leader, shows up to talk with
her, but gets mad when she brushes him
off in favor of Shinichi. Obviously,
he's jealous, so he goes to the school
and lures Shinichi into a nearby alley
for a conversation. Shinichi tries to
warn him, but Mitsuo picks up a fight.
>> It was at this moment that he knew
heeded up
>> while serving looks too. Is there
anything this guy can't do? Murano shows
up to catch the tail end of the fight
and the two of them walk home together.
She again brings up how different he
seems, especially since coming back from
his trip. Shinichi tries to play it off,
but there's no denying it. He is
different. Don't just take my word for
it either. The following morning, while
they're walking to school, Shinichi
saves a puppy getting hit by a car. Its
injuries are too severe to heal, so
Shinichi and Murano sit with it until it
dies. Then he throws it into a trash
can. I don't know about you, but that
gives me some serious ick. They're
literally at the park. He could have
buried the puppy. Murano also thinks
he's acting uncharacteristically cold
and leaves. Shinichi can't figure out
what he did wrong, which is a glaring
red flag if you ask me. He has to ask
Migi for help understanding the
situation. Migi, he goes back and buries
the puppy later, so he may not be
completely hopeless. Anyway, Shinichi
goes to school only for Murano to avoid
him. While he's there, he picks up on a
nonviolent parasite in the area and goes
to meet with him in a secluded
stairwell. That's where he meets the new
parasite from the beginning of the
episode, who introduces himself as
Shimada. He's kind of a jerk, instantly
showing off and breaking school records
during their gym class. He tells
Shinichi that he has no intention of
causing a stir at the school, that he
follows a similar diet to that of
humans, and that he wants to find a way
for parasites and humans to coexist. He
also reveals that Ryoko's going by a new
name, Reiko. Obviously, Shinichi doesn't
trust this guy, but he isn't going to
start a fight. After school, Kana bumps
into Shimada, thinking that he is
Shinichi. She incorrectly assumes that
she is sensing Shinichi because he's her
soulmate. Someone's a romantic, not
because he's part alien. Shimada tries
to drag Kana away so they can talk, but
Shinichi arrives just in time to save
her. He then walks Kana to the train
station and after hearing about her
unique sixth sense, warns her not to use
it as it'll lead her to bad people. In
the final moments of the episode, we get
to see Yuki again, who stays up late
making a lifelike portrait of Shimada.
Girl, you've got questionable taste in
men. Speaking of Shimada, we also see
him change his face, approach a lost
woman, and lure her into a secluded area
where he proceeds to eat her. So, we
can't trust anything that comes out of
this guy's mouth. Got it. The ninth
episode starts like an average day,
except Kana bumps into Shimada again. He
has his eye on her because of her sixth
sense, but can't hurt her because
they're in public and are interrupted by
Mitsuo. Obviously, he gets jealous of
Kana talking to another guy, so he tries
to start a fight. Shimada roughs him up
a bit, but lets him go before heading to
school. Yuko witnesses this from afar
because she's stalking her new crush.
After school, Shinichi comes home to
find his dad entertaining some guests.
It's a nice change of pace because
Kazuyuki has picked up a nasty drinking
habit and spends most nights down in a
bottle. Now he has three men in black
types confirming the existence of
parasites, confirming his wife's death,
and swearing Kazuyuki to secrecy to
avoid creating a mass panic. They even
have a profile artist there who drew an
accurate depiction of a parasite. After
the police leave, Kazuyuki tells
Shinichi what really happened to his
mom. Shinichi pretends to be surprised

Unfortunately, he doesn't get a clean
cut, which means Gooto can fight back.
Migi stays to fight, giving Shinichi the
chance to escape. With Migi gone,
Shinichi is totally alone and lost in
the woods. It's only a matter of time
before Gooto catches up with him and he
has nowhere to go. More importantly, he
only has one arm now and he's wounded.
He comes upon a house in the middle of
the night and the resident, a lonely
woman named Mitsuya, brings him in,
cleans him up, and gives him a place to
stay for a few days. She needs his
company as much as he needs hers, and
she manages to put some pep in his step
before he goes home. Unfortunately, on
the day he'll leave to go home, men from
Msio's village come to her door because
of a murder in the woods. They try to
blame Shinichi for the murder. But when
they go to look at the body, they
realize he couldn't be responsible.
Shinichi, knowing Gooto is now closing
in on the innocent village, goes out on
his own to finish what he started. His
lone weapon is a wood axe he takes from
Mitsuyo's place. In the 23rd episode,
Shinichi goes out to meet his maker. He
actually finds Goto in the woods, curled
up in a ball and asleep. In his
vulnerable state, Shinichi doesn't
hesitate to bring the wood cleaver down
on his neck. Unfortunately, Goto wakes
up in time to deflect the attack,
sending Shinichi on the defensive.
Hidden in the woods, Shinichi knows the
odds are stacked against him. Without
Migi, he doesn't stand a chance against
the strong parasite. That doesn't stop
him from trying, though. Shinichi is a
fighter, so he sharpens a stick and
climbs a tree, hoping to jab it through
a crack in Gooto's armor. Instead, he
gets caught midair and thrown into a
nearby tree. Things aren't looking good,
and he's definitely going to get himself
killed, but he has a rusty pipe and a
dream. He finds a pipe sticking out of
the ground, remembering a spot on
Gooto's side where he bled from a
gunshot wound and drives it in. His plan
actually works. He harms Goto, which
only makes him angrier. Goto goes to
land a killing blow, but Migi, revealed
to have been fused with Gooto's body,
and the other parasites inside it, stop
the attack. Then he detaches from Gooto
to reconnect with the residual parts of
him inside of Shinichi. Migi's back and
he's there to fight. When Migi leaves
Goto's body, the other parasites also
start pulling apart until his body
eventually bursts, further weakened by
Shinichi unknowingly poisoning him with
that rusty pipe. As they watch Goto
struggle to stay alive, our guys have a
moral debate. Migi can't bring himself
to murder one of his own kind anymore.
And Shinichi thinks that showing mercy
is a human trait to aspire to. But then
he thinks of his friends and family,
their safety, and kills Goto for good.
In the season finale, things start to
change for the better. After the
military wiped out all the parasites at
city hall, and our guys took down Goto
once and for all, the parasites slowly
faded into a distant memory. While some
believe their species went extinct,
others think they went into hiding,
choosing to conform to society to
survive. Either way, the mincemeat
murders ended and there was no one left
for Shinichi to hunt down. With peace
restored, Migi comes to Shinichi in a
dream one night, telling him that he
plans to go to sleep, only permanently
this time to fade into Shinichi's
consciousness so he can rest and take in
information in the same way he did when
morphed with Gooto. Migi encourages
Shinichi to forget him and carry on with
his life, but you know that little alien
is Shinichi's ride or die. A year passes
and Shinichi goes to meet with Murano
for a date. He looks good, guys. More
like his normal self from the start of
the series, but also changed, too. More
importantly, he's obviously happy and
he's working towards taking the entrance
exams to get into college since chasing
down parasites meant him missing out the
first time. Life is great, it seems,
until he's walking through the city and
notices Uraami tailing him. Seriously,
it's the finale. We couldn't have a
happy epilogue. Anyway, Uragami lures
them into a building and then takes
Murano hostage on the roof where he
calls Shinichi out for not being human.
Apparently, he knew the whole time that
he was a parasite, albeit different from
the others. He goes on a spiel about how
he's the definition of human nature and
wants to know what Shinichi makes of
him. Shinichi couldn't care less about
this guy. He wants his girl. So Uragami
throws Murano off the roof. Shinichi
runs for it, punching Uragami in the
face and trying to catch Murano, but he
isn't fast enough. He collapses to his
knees, grieving the loss of his first
and only love. When I tell you I stopped
breathing. There's like two minutes left
to the episode and this is what they do
with it. That's when Migi tells Shinichi
to snap out of it because he's tired and
he can't keep holding her forever.
Shinichi opens his eyes and there he is
leaning over the building while holding
Murano's hand. Thank god. He hauls her
to safety and they hold one another as
Shinichi stares off into the open blue
sky. And that's the entire story of
Parasite the Maxim. For a series about
invasive aliens, I didn't expect the
emotional whirlwind it put me through.
Hopefully Shinichi can make it into
college without another intergalactic
incident getting in the way. Are you
looking for another anime to fill the
migysized hole in your heart? Make sure
to check out some of my other videos
that you see right here. And in the
meantime, peace.
